strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure, and AI-agent tooling
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective open-source geo-data platform (still active), early-stage startup experience at WatchUp
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
concerns: No recognized AI expertise at scale — AI work is limited to ~1 year of MCP tooling; no demonstrated AI products or platforms with measurable impact
Cloud experience is shallow for a cloud strategy role — AWS CDK at Optilyz and Kubernetes on a side project do not constitute cloud platform advisory depth
No evidence of director-level IC scope: advising strategic customers/partners, shaping multi-team technical strategy, or external thought leadership beyond one FOSS4G talk
response.justification: The role demands recognized AI expertise and cloud platform strategy advisory capabilities at a director-level IC scope. Szabo's 20-year career demonstrates genuine breadth — full-stack, embedded, high-throughput data pipelines, open-source platform ownership — and his current work at re:cinq does touch agentic AI infrastructure. However, the AI work is recent (~1 year) and scoped to developer tooling rather than AI products or platforms with demonstrated business impact, which the role explicitly requires. Cloud experience amounts to peripheral tool use (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) rather than cloud platform strategy. There is no evidence of advising strategic customers, shaping organizational technical direction, or external thought leadership at the scale expected of a Director-level IC. Education is a bachelor's only, versus the preferred Master's/PhD in an AI-related field. The candidate scores well on engineering longevity and entrepreneurial instinct but fails to meet the core competencies the role is built around.
